title: Hieracium epileion
properties: Hieracium epileion has been reported to have various therapeutic properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Research suggests that Hieracium epileion may also have anti-cancer properties, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant's antioxidant properties are believed to contribute to its potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Further studies are necessary to fully explore the therapeutic potential of Hieracium epileion.

intro: Hieracium epileion, also known as devil's-bit scabious, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium epileion include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with devil's-bit scabious include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and to promote overall well-being.
